employee.vulnerability: you are the more vulnerable party in this employment relationship

clause_frames.Non-Compete: ⚠️ As the employee, this clause means YOU cannot work in this industry for the specified period after leaving. This directly limits your career options and income potential.
clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🔴 The employer can terminate your employment at any time without cause. You have limited job security under this clause.
clause_frames.Ip Ownership Assignment: 🔴 Any work, inventions, or ideas you create during employment become the EMPLOYER's property — not yours. This includes side projects if broadly worded.
clause_frames.Indemnification: ⚠️ You may be required to personally cover the employer's losses. Check if this is mutual or one-sided.
clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🔴 There is no limit on how much you could owe. This is extremely risky for you as an individual.
clause_frames.Non-Disparagement: ⚠️ You cannot publicly criticize the employer, even after leaving. This limits your ability to warn others or leave honest reviews.
clause_frames.Exclusivity: 🔴 You cannot take on other work or clients while employed. This restricts your income sources.

employer.vulnerability: you hold more power in this relationship but still have obligations

clause_frames.Non-Compete: 🟢 This protects your business from employees joining competitors. Ensure it's reasonable in scope to be enforceable.
clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🟢 You retain flexibility to end employment. Standard clause in your favor.
clause_frames.Ip Ownership Assignment: 🟢 Work created by employees belongs to your company. Standard and protective.
clause_frames.Indemnification: 🟡 Ensure indemnification is appropriately scoped to protect the company.
clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🟡 Be aware this exposes the company to unlimited claims.

tenant.vulnerability: you have less negotiating power and are dependent on the property

clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🔴 If only the LANDLORD can terminate at will, you could lose your home with minimal notice. Check if you have the same right.
clause_frames.Renewal Term: ⚠️ Auto-renewal can lock you into another lease term if you miss the cancellation window. Mark the deadline in your calendar.
clause_frames.Notice Period To Terminate Renewal: ⚠️ You must give notice X days before the lease ends to avoid auto-renewal. Missing this deadline traps you in another term.
clause_frames.Indemnification: 🔴 You may be liable for ALL damages, repairs, and legal costs — even those not your fault. Push for mutual indemnification.
clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🔴 There is no limit on what you could owe for damages. Negotiate a reasonable cap.
clause_frames.Liquidated Damages: 🔴 Pre-set penalty amounts for breaking the lease. Check if these amounts are reasonable.

landlord.vulnerability: you own the property but must comply with tenant protection laws

clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🟢 You retain the right to end the lease. Ensure compliance with local tenant protection laws.
clause_frames.Indemnification: 🟢 Tenant indemnification protects your property investment.
clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🟢 Tenant assumes full liability. Standard in your favor.

service_provider.vulnerability: you are trading your skills for payment and need to protect your work and limit exposure

clause_frames.Ip Ownership Assignment: 🔴 ALL work you create under this contract becomes the CLIENT's property. You cannot reuse, showcase, or sell it. Negotiate to retain portfolio rights.
clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🔴 If something goes wrong, there is NO LIMIT on what you could owe. A single mistake could bankrupt you. ALWAYS negotiate a liability cap.
clause_frames.Indemnification: 🔴 You must cover the client's losses. Ensure this is mutual and reasonably scoped.
clause_frames.Non-Compete: 🔴 You cannot work with competing clients. This directly cuts your income — negotiate a narrow scope.
clause_frames.Exclusivity: 🔴 You can only work for THIS client. This eliminates all other income sources.
clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🔴 The client can end the contract at any time without paying for work in progress. Negotiate a kill fee.
clause_frames.Cap On Liability: 🟡 There's a cap, but check if it's reasonable relative to your fees.

client.vulnerability: you are paying for services and need to ensure quality and ownership

clause_frames.Ip Ownership Assignment: 🟢 Work created belongs to you. Standard and protective for clients.
clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🟢 The provider assumes full liability for their work.
clause_frames.Indemnification: 🟢 The provider covers your losses. Ensure this is clearly stated.
clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🟢 You can end the engagement if needs change. Flexible for you.

borrower.vulnerability: you are in a financially dependent position and need to understand all costs

clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🔴 No limit on penalties. Default could have devastating financial consequences.
clause_frames.Liquidated Damages: 🔴 Pre-set penalty for missed payments or default. Check if amounts are reasonable.
clause_frames.Indemnification: 🔴 You bear all costs if something goes wrong. Check the scope carefully.
clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🔴 The lender can call the loan at any time. This creates severe financial uncertainty.
clause_frames.Minimum Commitment: 🔴 You must borrow/repay a minimum amount. Check if you can prepay without penalty.

lender.vulnerability: you need to protect your investment and ensure repayment

clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🟢 Borrower assumes full liability. Standard protection for lenders.
clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🟢 You retain the right to call the loan if needed.

policyholder.vulnerability: you depend on this policy for protection when things go wrong

clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🔴 You may be liable for unlimited amounts not covered by insurance.
clause_frames.Indemnification: 🔴 You must cover the insurer's losses — this is backwards and very risky.
clause_frames.Covenant Not To Sue: 🔴 You WAIVE your right to sue the insurer. This removes your primary recourse if they deny a claim.
clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🔴 The insurer can cancel your policy at any time. You could lose coverage when you need it most.

user.vulnerability: you have virtually no negotiating power — it's take-it-or-leave-it

clause_frames.Covenant Not To Sue: 🔴 You WAIVE your right to sue. You can only go through their arbitration process.
clause_frames.Indemnification: 🔴 You must cover THEIR losses if they get sued because of your use of the platform.
clause_frames.Uncapped Liability: 🔴 No limit on what you could owe. Extremely risky.
clause_frames.Termination For Convenience: 🔴 They can terminate your account at any time, potentially losing your data.
clause_frames.Ip Ownership Assignment: 🔴 Content you create on the platform may become THEIR property. Read carefully.

general.vulnerability: review each clause carefully to understand your rights and obligations
